---
title: "Stop NTFS volume from mounting"
date: 2007-05-29
forum: General Help
---

### Post by Sand Lee on 2007-05-29
I'm trying to figure out  how to stop my ntfs volume from mounting. I don't think I need it any more and its icon always shows up on the desktop. It's just an annoyance.

---

### Post by taurus on 2007-05-29
Do you mount it from /etc/fstab?  Post your /etc/fstab.

Applications -> Accessories -> Terminal
```
cat /etc/fstab
```

---

### Post by Sand Lee on 2007-05-29
```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda1 :
UUID=9d6eb1c3-49c3-4a72-9fc9-ed9e3cd9733a / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda2 :
UUID=37c05343-2b64-46ac-b1f4-824927a24239 /storage ext3 defaults 0 2
# Entry for /dev/hda3 :
UUID=e78d44c6-7c7d-4bb2-82a6-7ac1497f197e none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hdd /media/cdrom1 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hdb2 /media/HP_PAVILION ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 0
```

I should mention that I'm using NTFS Config, but none of its options are selected (so no read/write for internal or external volume).

---

### Post by merlinus on 2007-05-29
Try commenting out the last line by putting a hash mark (#) in front of it.

-merlin

---

### Post by Sand Lee on 2007-05-29
Gee thanks!! It worked! :D Would you be ever so kind as to letting me know what commenting out that line did?

---

### Post by merlinus on 2007-05-29
That was the line telling ubuntu to mount your ntfs drive.

The hash mark told the system to ignore that line.

Glad it worked....

-merlin

---
